meta.source_document: Court Reserve Calendar — Business Decisions & Policies
meta.notes: Machine-readable expression of all business decisions governing automated recommendations and manual management of the court reserve calendar. Edit this file (not the code) when policies change.

fixed_events.note: Recurring weekly events that are always on the schedule. Used by the recommender for saturation calculations and to avoid recommending conflicting events. Courts and max_participants are informational.

utilization.note: Target = target_pct × (courts × window_hours). Multi-court events count N courts × duration hours toward utilization. Recommender fills to target where feasible under hard constraints.

weekday.note: Extended to 20:00 on 2026-04-07 to allow 6 PM event starts.

hard_constraints.precedence_order_note: Constraints are enforced in the order listed below (1 = highest priority).

1_no_same_court_overlap.description: No recommendation may occupy a time window on a court that overlaps any existing event on that court on the target day.

2_one_court_per_event.description: Each recommended event must be assigned to at least one court. Multi-court events are booked on a primary court then edited to include all courts in a single occurrence.

3_max_occurrences_per_event_per_day.description: No eventId may appear more than twice on the same day across all events.

4_required_level_coverage.description: Recommendations must collectively include all five levels when possible. Levels already well-covered by existing events are skipped.
4_required_level_coverage.saturation_threshold_note: If a level already has this many sessions on the target day (across all events, not just approved ones), it is considered covered and will not be forced in Pass 1. Set to 0 to always force all levels.

5_utilization_optimization.description: Generate enough non-conflicting events to reach the utilization target if feasible under constraints 1–4.

event_duration_hours.note: All Open Play sessions must be exactly 2 hours. This is a hard rule — do not recommend 1-hour open play slots.

preferred_block_duration_hours.note: All blocks are 2 hours. 1-hour fallback removed.

spread_throughout_day.description: Distribute recommendations evenly across morning, midday, afternoon, and evening rather than front-loading the earliest slots.

time_of_day_preference.description: When historical attendance is equal (or absent), noon–5 PM slots are preferred over early morning. Used as a tiebreaker in Pass 1 and Pass 2 slot ordering. Prevents stacking of 8 AM recommendations when the schedule has no history for those slots.

recommendation_rules.two_court_pair_note: For 2-court events, try pairs in priority order. Both courts in the pair must be free at the same time. If no pair is free, fall back to the best available single court.
recommendation_rules.target_date_note: When no date is passed to run.py, defaults to 14 days from today. Override by passing a date argument: python run.py M/D/YYYY

llm_option.description: Replaces rule-based Pass 1 and Pass 2 with a single Claude API call that reasons over all constraints simultaneously. Pass 0 (fixed events) always runs rule-based before the LLM call.
llm_option.fallback: If the API call fails for any reason (network, quota, key missing), the engine falls back to rule-based logic automatically and logs a warning.
llm_option.tool_use: Uses Claude tool_choice forced to 'book_slots' for structured output. Response is re-validated by _parse_bookings() to drop any hallucinated slots or event IDs.
llm_option.api_key_config: Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env at project root. load_dotenv(override=True) is required to ensure .env values overwrite any pre-existing empty shell env vars.

time_bands.note: Each band represents a 2-hour window starting at that hour. Finer granularity vs. the prior 3-hour bands reduces false ties and improves time-slot selection.

event_id_matching.fallback: title-based level detection — only applied to events whose title contains 'open play' (case-insensitive). Maps detected level to the canonical approved event ID for that level.
event_id_matching.reason: Historical records sometimes reference event IDs that differ from current approved IDs. Title matching recovers useful attendance data that would otherwise be lost.

history_analysis.note: History is fetched weekly and stored in history/. The recommender reads history/history_latest.json.

url_notes.EditEventOccurrence: Returns 404 — does not exist as a standalone page. Use UpdateReservation instead.
url_notes.occurrence_id: The 'Id' field returned by the schedule report API is the reservationId used in UpdateReservation. Same number, different name.
url_notes.EditOnlyCurrentOccurrence: Checkbox on UpdateReservation form — must be checked to edit only this date, not the full recurring series.

multi_court_workflow.description: Two-court events require two steps: (1) add the occurrence on the primary court, (2) immediately edit that occurrence to assign all courts and set MaxPeople.
multi_court_workflow.step_1: Book via AddEventOccurrence on the primary court only. Capture the occurrence_id from the URL or occurrences grid after saving.
multi_court_workflow.step_2: Navigate to UpdateReservation using the captured occurrence_id. Check EditOnlyCurrentOccurrence, set Courts multiselect to all court IDs, set MaxPeople to the configured limit, save.
multi_court_workflow.max_participants_note: MaxPeople = 8 (4 per court) for all 2-court open play events. This field is only available on the edit (UpdateReservation) form, not the add form.
multi_court_workflow.reason: Court Reserve does not expose MaxPeople on the AddEventOccurrence form. Editing after booking is the only way to set it.

max_participants.note: Only available on edit form. Set to 8 for 2-court events (4 per court).

results_discord_post.description: After every booking session, results (success/failure per event) are posted back to Discord immediately.

dry_run_discord_preview.description: When run with --dry-run, recommendations are posted to Discord immediately as a preview (labeled '👀 Preview — not booking') without waiting for an approval reply. Allows reviewing what would be booked before committing.

retry.description: If any bookings fail, Discord is prompted with retry instructions. User can reply 'retry', 'retry 1,2', or 'skip'. Capped at 3 total attempts.

reply_commands.retry 1,2: Retry specific failed events by number

verification.description: After booking (and after each retry), the live schedule is re-fetched and each booked event is verified.
verification.match_strategy: event_id + start_time — more reliable than court label string matching

statuses.confirmed: Event found at correct time on correct court
statuses.wrong_court: Event found at correct time but on different court — auto-fix triggered
statuses.no_court: Event found at correct time with no court assigned — auto-fix triggered
statuses.not_found: Event not on schedule at all — re-add triggered

auto_remediation.method: fix_event_court() — navigates directly to UpdateReservation using occurrence_id if known, falls back to grid search, falls back to re-add
auto_remediation.occurrence_id_source: Preferred: Id field from live schedule API. Fallback: captured from occurrences grid after initial booking.

cli.fix_command: python run.py fix <date> (--event-id ID | --name 'fragment') [--start 'H:MM AM'] --court N [--dry-run]
cli.fix_command_description: Manually update the court assignment for a specific event occurrence. Fetches live schedule to find the occurrence ID, then edits via UpdateReservation form.

flags.--book: Execute bookings in Court Reserve after generating recommendations. Without this flag, recommendations are printed (and Discord preview posted) but nothing is booked.
flags.--dry-run: Generate recommendations and post a Discord preview labeled '👀 Preview — not booking'. Does not book anything.
flags.--llm: Use the Claude API (claude-sonnet-4-6) for Pass 1 + Pass 2 recommendation selection instead of rule-based logic. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env. Falls back to rule-based automatically on failure.

project_layout.description: Directory structure as of 2026-04-11. Reorganized from root-dumping to structured subdirectories.

directories.history/: Historical schedule data fetched from Court Reserve. history_latest.json is the canonical input to the recommender.
directories.logs/: Runtime logs from booking sessions. JSON booking logs named booking_log_YYYY-MM-DD.json.
directories.logs/screenshots/: Playwright screenshots taken during booking and court-fix operations.
directories.cache/: Temporary browser profile and session data used by Playwright.
directories.scripts/: Shell scripts and launchd helpers for scheduling automation.
directories.ops/: Operational configuration files (launchd plists, cron, etc.).
directories.archive/: Retired or superseded files kept for reference.

core_files.recommender.py: Recommendation engine (Pass 0 rule-based + Pass 1/2 rule-based or LLM)
core_files.llm_ranker.py: Claude API integration for Pass 1 + Pass 2
core_files.book_event.py: Playwright automation for booking and editing Court Reserve occurrences
core_files.cr_client.py: Court Reserve API client (schedule fetch, history fetch)
core_files.discord_notify.py: Discord webhook integration for recommendations and approval flow
core_files.history_analysis.py: Historical attendance analysis and popularity scoring
core_files.fetch_history.py: Fetches 3-month history from Court Reserve and writes to history/
core_files.policy.json: Single source of truth for all business rules and scheduling policy

playwright_setup.browser: Persistent Chrome profile via launch_persistent_context for session persistence across runs.
playwright_setup.chrome_detection: Prefers system Chrome (detected via shutil.which('google-chrome') or 'chrome'). Falls back to Playwright's bundled Chromium if system Chrome is not installed.
playwright_setup.note: Using a persistent profile avoids re-authenticating to Court Reserve on every run.

fallback_behavior.when_utilization_unreachable: Stop at maximal feasible utilization under hard constraints. Report remaining gap explicitly in court-hours and percent points.
fallback_behavior.relaxation_order: TBD — requires explicit business approval before implementation. Suggested: relax constraint 5 first (utilization), then 4 (level coverage), never relax 1–3.

traceability.note: History is fetched weekly via fetch_history.py and stored locally. Scheduler run logs go to logs/.

governance.eventid_whitelist_change_policy: Approved event IDs may only be added or removed with explicit business approval. Update approved_events in this file; do not hardcode IDs in scheduler logic.
governance.operating_window_change_policy: Windows are configurable by day-of-week in this file.
governance.fixed_events_change_policy: Fixed events may only be added or removed with explicit business approval. Update fixed_events.events in this file.
governance.multi_court_max_participants_change_poli: MaxPeople limit for 2-court events is set in booking.multi_court_workflow.max_participants_per_two_court_event. Update here; do not hardcode in book_event.py.
governance.two_court_priority_change_policy: Court pair priority order is set in recommendation_rules.two_court_priority_pairs. Update here; do not hardcode in recommender.py.
governance.llm_model_change_policy: The Claude model used for LLM recommendations is set in recommendation_engine.llm_option.model. Update here and in llm_ranker.py MODEL constant together.
governance.version_control: This file should be tracked in version control. Git history serves as the audit log for policy changes.
